title: Ledger Touching Lab — Decanter

sections.body:
The laboratory report, written in the cramped hand of a chemist who despised policemen, concludes: Lead-white traces found in Sauval's glass only. The decanter is clean. The stopper bears fresh prints—Caillou's only. Whoever poisoned the glass added the compound directly to Sauval's pour, not to the decanter. The poison is basic lead carbonate, identical to that which Sauval used in his white paint. The dose would have produced symptoms within an hour and death within three to six, depending on the victim's constitution.

Trust this sheet if thou wilt; the sergeant trusted it not, yet kept it. A prudent reader will note what is said, and more keenly what is artfully left unsaid. The desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers."

A crabbed clerk's remembrance was left below the fold: fifth bell, third stair, third lantern, seventh berth.

subtitle: Entered as sober truth, though truth oft weareth a counterfeit coat.
